balance_sheet.title: Commentary on Balance Sheet Overview: As of May 31, 2026
balance_sheet.content:
The investment securities portfolio expanded by $4.58 million to reach a balance of $39.27 million, representing the primary driver behind total assets growing 7.8% year-over-year to $63.65 million.

This increase in investments broadened the earning-asset base outside lending and now accounts for 61.7% of all assets. Concurrently, cash deposits rose by $2.57 million to $3.60 million, improving near-term liquidity as funds moved into this category despite loans declining by $2.47 million.

The asset growth was matched on the funding side through a reduction in shares deposited, which fell 6.3% or $3.17 million to $47.13 million. This contraction in deposits provided the necessary balance-sheet identity support for the net increase in assets and investments while other liabilities decreased by $2.79 million.

Total liabilities declined 5.5% year-over-year, driven primarily by the reduction in shares deposited which serves as the institution's main funding source. Accrued liabilities increased by $343,560 to $820,726 but remain a minor component of the overall liability structure.

Total equity stood at $15.71 million with undivided earnings matching that figure and reserves not disclosed in this period. The combined asset expansion through investments alongside stable capital levels indicates an improved earning-asset positioning supported by reduced deposit reliance, while monitoring should focus on whether continued investment growth can offset the decline in loan balances.

earning.title: Commentary on Earnings: As of May 31, 2026
earning.content:
DATA CHECK: non-interest income segments sum to $2.19 million while the stated total is $61K; DATA CHECK: non-interest expense segments sum to $516K while the stated total is $560K.

Net Income for Bopti Federal Credit Union was $265,377 in Q2 2026. The primary earnings driver remained net interest income of $785,891 ($1,178,992 minus $393,101), which provided the foundation for profitability this period.

Non-interest income totaled $61,283 and served as a modest secondary contributor to overall earnings. Non-interest expense was $560,366, with compensation costs representing the dominant category within that total.

Overall Position: The credit union generated net income of $265,377 by leveraging its core interest spread against non-interest expenses totaling $560,366. Sustaining profitability will depend on maintaining this positive relationship between net interest income and operating costs.

key_financial.title: Commentary on Key Financial Performance: As of May 31, 2026
key_financial.content:
Capital and solvency metrics for Bopti Federal Credit Union as of the reporting date show a net worth ratio at 24.67%, an increase of 253 basis points from the prior period end. The credit union maintains this level of capital.

Asset quality indicators reflect mixed movements in the current environment. The delinquency ratio increased to 1.73% following a change of 35 basis points, while the net charge-off ratio decreased to 0.50%, reflecting an improvement of 62 basis points from the prior period end.

Profitability results for this period include return on average assets at 0.95%. Net interest margin stands at 2.85% with a change of 28.52 basis points, and net operating expense ratio is recorded at 1.91%, showing an increase of 14.90 basis points from the prior period end.

Balance sheet positioning shows loans-to-shares ratio at 42.25%. Loan balance % change reflects a decrease of -27.52% and shares/deposits % change is recorded as -11.03%, indicating contraction in these areas during this reporting period.

The credit union's financial position across all four areas presents mixed results, with capital strength offsetting softer growth conditions alongside rising delinquency but improving charge-offs. Continued monitoring of the delinquency ratio will be important given its upward movement.

loan.title: Commentary on Loan Condition: As of May 31, 2026
loan.content:
Total loans stood at $19.9 million as of May 31, 2026, reflecting a decrease of 11.5 percent from the prior period. The portfolio is concentrated in used vehicle lending and new vehicle lending, which together represent 62.1 percent of total assets. Delinquent loans increased by 1.3 percent to $578,594 during this same timeframe. Management continues to focus on credit quality through underwriting standards and ongoing monitoring.

Portfolio Composition
Used vehicle loans totaled $6.7 million or 33.9 percent of the portfolio, while new vehicle loans comprised $5.6 million or 28.2 percent. These two segments combined account for 62.1 percent of total loan balances. Unsecured lending represented $5.0 million or 25.4 percent of assets, other secured non-revolving credit totaled $1.4 million at 6.9 percent, and first lien secured loans made up the remaining balance.

Delinquency & Credit Quality
Total delinquent loans reached $578,594 with a year-over-year increase of 1.3 percent. Unsecured lending accounted for $256,431 in delinquencies, followed by used vehicle loans at $192,016 and other secured non-revolving credit at $68,094.

The largest delinquency exposure sits in unsecured lending, which represents the single highest dollar amount among all segments. This contrasts with portfolio concentration where used vehicle and new vehicle lending dominate total assets but represent smaller shares of current delinquent balances compared to their overall size.

loan_continue.title: Charge-offs & Recoveries; Allowance & CECL Position; Overall Assessment (March 31, 2026)
loan_continue.content:
DATA CHECK: recoveries of $21K exceed gross charge-offs of $65K for the March 31, 2026 period.

Charge-Offs & Recoveries
Gross charge-offs totaled $65K during the March 31, 2026 period. Losses were concentrated in unsecured loans at $65K and used vehicles at $30. Recovery activity amounted to $21K across all segments. Unsecured recoveries contributed $12K while new vehicle recoverings totaled $359 and used vehicle recoveries reached $8K.

Management maintains a continued focus on collections efforts and portfolio monitoring throughout the March 31, 2026 period.

Allowance & CECL Position
The CECL allowance declined to $122M with a percentage change of -47%. This decline occurred alongside reduced gross charge-offs during the same reporting window. Reserve levels address:
- Risk concentrations within unsecured and used vehicle portfolios
- Current delinquency and charge-off trends
- Expected future credit conditions and portfolio performance
Management continues its ongoing evaluation of reserve adequacy.

Overall Assessment
Credit risk remains stable for this period. Gross charge-offs decreased while the allowance balance also declined during the March 31, 2026 reporting window. Capital and reserve coverage remain adequate to support current operations. Management maintains an ongoing focus on underwriting standards and portfolio monitoring.

share.title: Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of May 31, 2026
share.content:
Portfolio Composition
Member shares totaled $47.1 million as of the reporting date, reflecting a decline of 6.3 percent over the prior period. The overall contraction was driven by reductions across nearly all major share categories.

Regular shares represented the largest funding component at $27.6 million or 57.2 percent of total member equity. Share certificates followed as the second-largest category with balances of $11.7 million, accounting for 24.2 percent of the portfolio. IRA and Keogh accounts comprised a meaningful portion of the mix at $7.1 million, representing 14.8 percent of the total.

All major categories declined during this period: regular shares decreased by 3.5 percent, share certificates fell by 3.4 percent, and IRA/Keogh balances dropped by 8.4 percent. Share drafts also contracted slightly at a rate of 1.0 percent. The combination of declines across both flexible regular shares and fixed-term certificates indicates that the funding mix is moving toward less growth while maintaining its existing cost structure.

The period's decline in member equity reduces overall leverage but preserves the institution's ability to manage liquidity through established relationships with members who hold share drafts, IRA/Keogh accounts, and term products. Management continues to focus on retaining these long-standing member relationships while monitoring funding costs.

investment.title: Commentary on Investments: As of May 31, 2026
investment.content:
Portfolio Composition
Total investments stood at $39.4 million as of the reporting date, reflecting a year-over-year increase of 13.2%. Held-to-Maturity Debt Securities comprised 99.7% of this total balance and provided scheduled cash flows through maturity.
Other Investments represented the remaining portion of the portfolio with a value of $109,776 that remained unchanged from the prior period.
The Investment Maturity Distribution
The investment maturity distribution chart displays holdings across multiple time horizons including short-term buckets under one year as well as longer-dated categories extending beyond ten years. This spread may support liquidity needs by providing access to funds at various points in the future while maintaining a diversified structure.
Overall Liquidity Position and Monitoring Focus
This portfolio composition, characterized by its heavy concentration in held-to-maturity debt securities with a maturity distribution spanning multiple horizons, supports the institution's overall liquidity position. The share of investments held in longer-term buckets relative to total holdings warrants ongoing monitoring.

membership.title: Commentary on Members: As of March 31, 2026
membership.content:
The credit union's membership count remained flat quarter over quarter as of March 31, 2026.

Members per full-time employees trended slightly upward from year-end 2025 through the first quarter of 2026. This indicates that staffing levels are aligned with service demand for current members.

Shares per member declined during this period. Existing members hold a smaller average deposit balance, which affects the funding base per member even if total membership is unchanged; this decline occurred while the number of current members remained stable quarter over quarter.

The credit union maintained flat headcount and rising efficiency in Q1 2026 despite lower deposits-per-member. Continued focus on engagement and retention will support deposit growth tied to the specific consequence identified above.

policy.title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: As of May 31, 2026
policy.content:
The credit union is largely compliant with policy limits across profitability and capital categories. Asset quality remains within acceptable thresholds for classified assets and charge-offs; however, delinquency has exceeded its limit. Liquidity metrics show multiple exceptions to range requirements, while funding costs are controlled despite a shortfall in net interest margin targets.

Asset Quality
Classified assets remained below the 40% threshold at -0.78%, indicating strong asset quality relative to this specific metric; however, delinquency breached its limit by reaching 1.73% against a ceiling of 1.5%. Net charge-offs stayed within limits at 0.5%.

Capital & Solvency
Net worth stood at 24.67%, well above the 10% floor; however, net worth growth trailed its target by reaching only 4.12% against a minimum of 5%. The solvency evaluation remained within targets at 133.33%.

Profitability & Growth
Net margin achieved 2.95%, exceeding the 1.00% floor; however, average assets net interest income fell short of its target by reaching only 2.85% against a guideline of ≥ 3.00%. Return on average assets remained within targets at 0.95%. Loan growth contracted to -27.52%, missing the 5%-10% range; however, market share and membership metrics also declined significantly.

Interest Rate & Funding
Cost of funds stayed within limits at 1.41%; however, average assets net interest margin fell below its target by reaching only 2.85%. Average assets yield on loans remained strong at 8.25% against a floor of ≥ 4.00%.

Liquidity & Mix
Cash and short-term investments stood at 5.66%, falling outside the required range; however, regular shares also dropped to 57.7% below its target threshold. Total loans represented only 31.28% of total assets against a benchmark of 65%-80%; additionally, total loans as a percentage of average assets reached just 42.25%, missing the 70%-90% range.

The credit union maintains robust capitalization and controlled funding costs but faces notable compliance gaps in liquidity mix requirements and asset quality delinquency limits. Management will prioritize restoring regular share levels to meet regulatory thresholds while monitoring delinquency trends closely.

ceo_summary.title: CEO Summary: Operational & Strategic Highlights (As of May 31, 2026)
ceo_summary.content:
Bopti Federal Credit Union maintains a strong capital position with a net worth ratio at 24.67%, significantly exceeding the regulatory well-capitalized threshold, while solvency stands at 133.33% to support earnings variability. Profitability remains within target for return on average assets at 0.95%, though net interest margin of 2.85% falls below its benchmark guideline due to a shift in earning-asset mix away from lending toward investments and cash deposits.

Total assets grew 7.8% year-over-year to $63.65 million, driven primarily by an investment portfolio expansion that added $4.58 million while loans contracted by $2.47 million or 11.5 percent. This structural shift broadened the earning-asset base outside lending so investments now account for 61.7% of all assets but reduced total loans to only 31.28% of average assets, falling below liquidity mix requirements and impacting net interest income generation.

Asset quality indicators show mixed results with a delinquency ratio increasing to 1.73%, which exceeds the policy threshold of 1.50%. Net charge-offs improved to 0.50% within limits, yet classified assets sit at -0.78% relative to net worth while credit stress concentrates in unsecured lending and used vehicle segments where delinquent balances reached $256,431 and $192,016 respectively.

Funding stability faces headwinds as member shares declined by 6.3 percent or $3.17 million to reach a balance of $47.13 million despite cost of funds remaining within limits at 1.41%. Membership count remained flat quarter over quarter, but the contraction in deposits across nearly all major share categories reduces overall leverage and requires monitoring whether continued investment growth can offset this decline in loan balances while preserving established member relationships.

Management will prioritize restoring regular share levels to meet regulatory thresholds alongside ongoing evaluation of reserve adequacy for risk concentrations within unsecured and used vehicle portfolios. Continued efforts will target reducing delinquency specifically in the segments identified as highest dollar exposure, improve asset mix deployment to address liquidity ratio gaps, and maintain capital discipline while managing earnings variability against softer growth conditions.

action_recommended.title: CEO's Actionable Recommendations (6-12 Months)
action_recommended.content:
1. Credit Quality And Collections Strategy
- Strengthen collections on unsecured lending where delinquent balances reached $256,431 to address the segment driving exposure above policy limits.
- Review underwriting standards for used vehicle loans given that this segment holds $192,016 in current delinquencies and represents a significant portion of total assets.
- Monitor net charge-off trends closely while maintaining allowance balance levels that address risk concentrations within unsecured and used vehicle portfolios identified in recent reporting.

2. Loan Expansion And Asset Utilization Plan
- Pursue lending growth initiatives to restore total loans as a percentage of average assets from current 42.25% toward the benchmark range of 70%-90%.
- Optimize asset mix deployment by reducing reliance on investments which now account for 61.7% of all assets to improve net interest income generation potential.
- Expand secured lending opportunities to offset the contraction in loan balances that decreased $2.47 million or 11.5 percent year-over-year during this reporting period.

3. Funding Stability And Membership Retention
- Restore regular share levels as they currently stand at 57.2% of total member equity which is below the established target threshold identified in policy limits.
- Strengthen retention efforts for members holding IRA and Keogh accounts given that these balances dropped by 8.4 percent while representing a meaningful portion of mix at $7.1 million.
- Maintain cost discipline on funding sources as shares per member declined during this period, ensuring overall leverage is preserved despite reductions across nearly all major share categories.

4. Net Interest Margin Improvement Goals
- Improve net interest margin performance to reach the guideline of ≥3.00% by rebalancing earning-asset mix away from investments which currently account for a high percentage of total assets.
- Maintain non-interest income sources at $61,283 while seeking opportunities to expand this modest secondary contributor against operating costs totaling $560,366.
- Monitor return on average assets performance which stood at 0.95% during Q2 2026 to ensure it continues meeting the established targets for profitability results.

5. Capital And Liquidity Management Actions
- Adjust liquidity metrics by increasing cash and short-term investments which currently stand at 5.66%, falling outside the required range identified in policy limits.
- Maintain net worth ratio discipline as it stands at 24.67% significantly exceeding regulatory thresholds while monitoring growth against targets of minimum 5%.
- Review solvency evaluation metrics which remained within targets at 133.33%, ensuring capital levels support earnings variability amidst softer growth conditions and asset quality indicators reflecting mixed movements.
